name: DeepSeek-V4-Pro (Think High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (1.6T total / 49B active), hybrid Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention, Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections, Muon optimizer
note: Fast/low-latency mode of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro preview checkpoint, the default reasoning tier a user gets without selecting anything. (middle reasoning-effort tier, called 'Think High' on the official HF model-card benchmark table).

results.notes: Think-High mode, per macaron.im's transcription of DeepSeek's technical report
Terminal-Bench 2.0, Think High mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).
BrowseComp Pass@1, Think High mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 12.4% / Score 27.6% (rank 40, listed as 'deepseek-v4-pro'). Mapped to the 'Think High' catalog variant (vs. 'Non-Think' or 'Think Max') as the closest match to the leaderboard's 'High' effort label -- flagged as a moderate-confidence inference since the leaderboard doesn't use DeepSeek's own 'Think High' naming verbatim.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real via DeepSeek's own official API-docs news post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news260424/, dated 2026-04-24) announcing the V4-Pro/V4-Flash preview, and via the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro Hugging Face model card, which gives 1.6T total/49B active params, MIT license, 1M-token context, and a benchmark table covering three reasoning tiers (Non-Think/Think High/Think Max). Numerous SEO/content-farm sites (codersera, sitepoint, morphllm, etc.) published speculative-sounding 'release timeline' articles with suspiciously precise but unverifiable claims (e.g. percentile rankings, an 'Intelligence Index' score) — those were NOT used; all numbers here come from the official HF model card table.
